title: Bangladesh ensuring import of refined fuel from alternative sources
summary:
Bangladesh’s Energy Division recently said the capacity of Eastern Refinery Limited (ERL) would affect little the fuel supply system as the unit contributes only a fifth of the country’s petroleum supply system while the rest is imported in refined form.
It has ensured import of refined fuel from alternative sources, and there will be no adverse impact on oil supply due to ERL’s low feed operations.

title: Modern Syntex to invest $7.5 mn in Bangladesh NSEZ expansion
summary:
Modern Syntex Limited will invest $7.5 million to expand high-value polyester yarn production at Bangladesh’s NSEZ in Mirsarai, with BEZA signing a land lease agreement. 
The project covers a 3.75-acre site. 
BEZA highlighted the role of domestic investment in reducing import reliance, while the expansion will use advanced, eco-friendly technologies to boost capacity and serve global markets.

title: ICE cotton extends rally on rising crude oil, US drought fears
summary:
ICE cotton futures extended their rally for a fifteenth session, supported by higher crude oil and drought concerns in the US. 
Prices hit multi-month highs, though trading volumes declined and stocks rose. 
Despite strong technical momentum, weak export demand and speculative liquidation signal underlying fragility and potential volatility.

title: Asahi Kasei Advance to showcase impactful fibres at Techtextil 2026
summary:
Asahi Kasei Advance will debut at Techtextil 2026, presenting 14 high-performance nonwovens and fibre solutions. 
Highlights include LASTAN flame-retardant fabric, Cubit recyclable 3D spacer, Bemliese biodegradable cellulose sheet, and ELTAS binder-free spunbond, serving safety, hygiene, automotive, filtration, and industrial applications across global markets at its debut showcase in Frankfurt.

title: Euro area annual inflation rises to 2.6% in March, EU at 2.8%
summary:
Euro area inflation rose to 2.6 per cent in March 2026 from 1.9 per cent in February, while EU inflation reached 2.8 per cent, according to the Eurostat. 
Prices increased in most member states, led by services and energy. 
Romania recorded the highest rate at 9 per cent, while Denmark saw the lowest at 1 per cent, signalling renewed inflationary pressure across the region.

title: Sangeet Syntex showcases innovations at FABTEX 2026
summary:
Sangeet Syntex Limited is showcasing its latest knitted fabric innovations at FABTEX 2026 in Mumbai from April 16–18.
The display includes greige and finished fabrics, performance finishes, and B2B garment solutions, highlighting its technical expertise, customisation strengths, and integrated manufacturing capabilities while engaging with industry stakeholders.

title: Messe Frankfurt unveils Val:ue Milan boutique hub for February 2027
summary:
Messe Frankfurt unveils Val:ue Milan, a boutique business hub for high-end apparel textiles, set for February 2027. 
Focused on curated excellence, it connects top global manufacturers with premium buyers in Milan. 
The concept strengthens Texpertise, offering a selective, high-impact platform for luxury sourcing and strategic partnerships.

title: Germany’s manufacturing hubs remain highly uncertain in March: Survey
summary:
Business uncertainty in Germany rose in March, with manufacturing sector remained highly affected with 87.7 per cent, according to ifo. 
While 78.6 per cent of total firms in the country struggle to assess future prospects, up from 75.4 per cent in February. 
Energy-intensive sectors faced the greatest pressure with 95 per cent affected. 
Services and trade also saw rising uncertainty.

title: US T&A imports volume falls 15.4% in Jan–Feb; exports decline
summary:
US textile and apparel imports fell 15.40 per cent in January–February 2026 to 14,173.67 million SME, with apparel and textiles both declining sharply. 
Cotton and MMF imports also dropped. 
Exports decreased by 9.83 per cent, though apparel shipments rose. 
The trend reflects continued demand weakness after a softer 2025 and volatile recovery in 2024.

title: US small business optimism falls in March 2026: NFIB survey
summary:
The US National Federation of Independent Business small business optimism index fell by 3 points in March to 95.8, leaving it below its 52-year average of 98. 
The seasonally-adjusted percentage of owners expecting better business conditions fell by 7 points from February to 11 per cent—the third monthly decline in a row. 
Sixteen per cent of them plan to make capital outlays in the next six months.

title: Revoking China PNTR may lead to higher tariffs borne by US firms: AAFA
summary:
US trade body AAFA has urged the International Trade Commission not to revoke the permanent normal trade relations (PNTR) status granted to China as that would result in higher tariffs borne by US companies. 
Higher tariffs on Chinese imports would constrain US firms’ ability to invest in innovation, expand operations and support US job growth, and would risk closing off commercial opportunities in China.

title: India’s T&A exports fall 14% in March, FY26 down 2.21%
summary:
India’s textile and apparel exports fell sharply in March 2026, declining 14.02 per cent year on year (YoY) to $2.91 billion amid geopolitical disruptions. 
Apparels dropped 18.99 per cent and textiles 9.91 per cent. 
FY 2025–26 exports slipped 2.21 per cent, with sector share easing to 8.10 per cent. 
Cotton imports surged annually, while MMF trends remained relatively stable.

title: US CBP to soon launch electronic system for importers to claim refunds
summary:
US Customs and Border Protection will launch on April 20 an electronic system that importers can use to claim tariff refunds authorised by court order or applicable law. 
Phase 1 will be limited to certain unliquidated entries and certain entries within 80 days of liquidation.
Refunds will be issued within 60-90 days of the Consolidated Administration and Processing of Entries declaration getting accepted.

title: Bangladesh RMG units call for allowing local FOC raw material sourcing
summary:
Bangladesh RMG players have urged the government to remove restrictions on sourcing raw materials free of cost (FOC) from local suppliers. 
Industry leaders said allowing FOC sourcing from domestic suppliers would boost local sales, strengthen backward linkage industries and raise overall value addition in the export-oriented sector. 
Many local suppliers can provide inputs now against global orders.

title: UK’s Sosandar returns to profitability amid robust FY26 performance
summary:
Sosandar reported FY26 revenue of £42.3 million (~$57.53 million), up 14 per cent, driven by strong online growth, with own-site sales rising 24 per cent. 
The company returned to profitability with PBT of £0.4 million (~$0.54 million) and improved margins. 
Despite slightly missing revenue expectations, performance remained solid. 
Strong third-party sales supported confidence in profitable growth.

title: Germany’s Puma appoints James Carnes to new creative leadership role
summary:
Puma has appointed James Carnes as senior vice president creative direction.
Reporting to Maria Valdes, he will oversee creative direction, innovation, and product excellence. 
With over two decades of experience, including leadership roles at Adidas, he will align design strategy with business goals to strengthen Puma’s global brand appeal and market position.

title: Ireland’s Primark boosts circular fashion with updated strategy
summary:
Primark has launched Circular Product Standard 2.0 to expand circular fashion across its range. 
The framework focuses on durability, sustainable materials and recyclability, covering nine product categories. 
In FY 24/25, 5 per cent of products were circular by design, including 20 per cent of jersey. 
A new ‘Progressive’ level raises standards, backed by training and practical design improvements.

title: Hong Kong apparel imports stabilise after sharp dip last year
summary:
Hong Kong’s apparel imports showed early stabilisation in January–February 2026, rising 0.7 per cent YoY to $946.49 million after a steep 2025 decline. 
Casualwear led gains, with T-shirts and jerseys increasing share, while structured wear softened. 
Despite this uptick, imports remain well below 2024 levels, reflecting cautious sourcing and weak global demand.

title: FTAs, PTAs in focus as Sri Lanka aims for growth
summary:
Industry stakeholders highlight that Sri Lanka faces a competitive disadvantage in global markets due to limited preferential trade agreements, unlike many competitors, making securing free trade agreements (FTAs) and preferential trade agreements (PTAs) critical. 
Industry stakeholders call upon the government to accelerate negotiations on FTAs and PTAs.

title: Italy’s apparel export-import plunge after positive trend in 2025
summary:
Italy’s sharp decline in apparel trade in early 2026 reflects short-term demand caution and inventory tightening rather than structural weakness.
Strong 2025 performance highlights resilient trade fundamentals supported by diversified markets.
Gradual sourcing shifts, including reduced reliance on China, indicate evolving supply chain strategies.
